articles.title: Q14 — Cognitive concerns
content: Does the elder have memory loss, confusion, or cognitive concerns?

ul: **Severe confusion (3)** — major cognitive impairment affecting safety

content: **Sudden** confusion (over days) is usually a treatable illness — UTI, medicine reaction, low B12, thyroid. **Slow** confusion (over months/years) is more likely dementia. Knowing which changes everything you do.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Family safety briefing (wandering, stove, medications, money); identification details on elder's person (band or card); cognitive engagement at centre if mobile
**Critical flag review.** Safety-proofing of home; constant supervision arrangement; medical referral to rule out reversible causes (UTI, medication effects, B12 deficiency)

articles.title: Q15a — Depression screen [Sensitive]
content: **Ask:** 'In the last 2 weeks, has the elder felt down, depressed, or hopeless, or had little interest or pleasure in doing things?' Ask **privately** where possible — not in front of family members who may be implicated.

ul: **Neither (0)** — neither low mood nor loss of interest observed
**Sometimes (1)** — symptoms present some days; not predominant
**Nearly every day (3)** — symptoms severe and persistent — **CRITICAL FLAG**

content: The 2-week anchor (from validated PHQ-2 screen) forces a thought about pattern, not appearance. Depression in slum elders is heavily underdiagnosed and treatable.

head: Response
Operational definition
rows: Neither low mood nor loss of interest observed.
Symptoms present some days; not predominant.
Symptoms severe and persistent — CRITICAL FLAG.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Mental health referral (PHC counsellor / NIMHANS DMHP / NGO partner); follow-up to confirm referral kept; centre engagement to address isolation

**Critical flag — severe depression.** Same-day supervisor brief; mental health referral within 14 days; weekly check-ins until stable; rule out self-harm risk through gentle conversation, not formal screening

articles.title: Q15b — Loneliness / social isolation [Sensitive]
content: **Ask:** 'In the last 2 weeks, has the elder felt lonely, or gone days without meaningful conversation with anyone?'

ul: **No, regular contact (0)** — regular social contact; no loneliness reported
**Occasionally (1)** — occasional loneliness; meaningful contact most days
**Often (2)** — frequent loneliness or isolation; meaningful contact rare
**Severely disconnected (3)** — no meaningful conversation in days; profoundly isolated

head: Response
Operational definition
rows: Regular social contact; no loneliness reported.
Occasional loneliness; meaningful contact most days.
Frequent loneliness or isolation; meaningful contact rare.
No meaningful conversation in days; profoundly isolated.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Centre enrolment (visit at least twice a week if mobile); peer mentor pairing; family conversation about visit/call frequency
Centre or community kitchen daily attendance; settlement-level connection to one neighbour or community member; weekly home visits intensified
